author: Gwailow
msg: I have a range of splits on the markeplace - 50/50 probably the highest amount for UNO at the moment, lowest is 12%.  For ULOs where I have a relationship I am doing 60/40 in ULO favour - a kind of early bird offer for people who help me get started, provide feedback etc. On the marketplace, its just transactional so whilst supply > demand, think they'll only lease at lower levels. However lower levels shouldnt be looked at negatively imo - it allows people to test out unity which is good for the overall project and also better to make a few cents a day than nothing at all.  UNO splits will increase as demand increases.  Just my view

author: Bitdano
msg: ULOS Have no skin in the game..profits only

author: Carlos
msg: But you need ULO’s to make money.

author: Bitdano
msg: Unity sells itself.. When this project starts cranking...licenses sell out quick

author: Gwailow
msg:
yep.. only 3 levers at the moment for UNOs in the marketplace
- ULO split
- Uptime requirement (not enforced so meaningless atm)
- Lease length (not enforced so meaningless atm)

So always going to be better deals for ULO until demand increases and/or you can build a value based relationship with your ULOs

author: Mat
msg: I get it with the very low UNO share to start with but I don't want to see UNO revoke these the minute the rewards go up as that's not fair on ULO's hence even I go for 40/60 in favor of the ULO and a 1month lease. Just how I'm doing it. Everyone will have their own plan.

author: Gwailow
msg: I agree and ideally this would be the path that most follow. I am doing 3 month leases at lower rates. 12 month leases for ULOs that I have a relationship with (60/40 split). Naturally UNO shares will increase with demand so maybe next time the leases are renewed they'll be at a higher UNO share (hopefully). Id still rather have someone use the licenses than they sit idle

author: Opher
msg:
I currently run 64 Devices dispersed in 6 different locations. I make .20 - .35 a day per device on average and I’ll make between $12-$19 a day/ $4380 - $6935 a year if current rewards sustain themselves. I’ll keep buying and dispersing. I’ll have 60 more devices dispersed by the end of January, so that’ll be 124 devices in total that I’ll operate in multiple locations. 
124devices @ .20-.35 a day is $24.8- $43.75 or $9,125- $15,968 a year.

author: Opher
msg: I mean there’s definitely opportunity to make money even with lower end rewards.

author: Carlos
msg: This works because you have about 10 devices per location which is what I have at home and get similar to what you get. Up the number to 20 or more devices and you won’t get the same. Farmers boxes have 20 devices per box.

author: Mat
msg:
Ok, as we don't have a roadmap or any real dates, for my own sanity and a bit of fun here are my predictions on dates.
1. Roadmap 20 Feb
2. iOS PoW 26 Jan
3. S&R first week of Feb for Alpha, mid March for general release.
4. New stand alone passive tasks Valentine's day but limited release and lottery to start with.
So lots to look forward to, will just take a little time.

msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - General** **Updates**

**Scout & Runner:** Final preparation underway. Alpha registration to be released in the next 48 hours, and further details to be announced this week.

**January Tasks: **Additional task types are in development alongside S&R. Updates will follow as each reaches deployment readiness.

**iOS Proof of Work:** In active development. Timeline to be confirmed shortly.

**Rewards: **Dynamic based on network demand and task availability. As new tasks deploy, opportunity expands.

**Round 1 Privilege: **Details will be communicated before the 4 February close.

Further information will be shared in the upcoming roadmap release. Secure your Unity Node at unitynodes.io to participate in the people-powered edge network.

**January Tasks: **Additional task types are in development alongside S&R. Updates will follow as each reaches deployment readiness.

msg:
I do not disagree on that. I never said that they do not work (or that they work crazy hours either). I do not have validated input on how big the team is or how many hours they are working. I am only validating results here. I hope they will deliver. I am not sure they will.

My argument here is regarding announcements and wording. I wouldn't complain if we got, for example, a delay update on "January tasks". I do not mind delays on an individual front - as long as I see overall progress.

They need to drastically improve their wording on announcements.

author: BeamNode
msg:
#ama

What if someone uses a rooted Android phone to clone the Unity app ten times on a single device? That would lock up ten licenses for months due to the contract.

Does Unity actually detect this? And if it does, can those devices truly be banned? Otherwise, revoking a license just means they take another one. (Or lower the rewards to $0)

If Unity does not detect this at all, how is a UNO supposed to detect that multiple licenses are being used through cloned Unity apps on a single phone?
